one_liner: iPayX provides an FX forensic-audit MCP server (free, mostly no-signup) plus an underlying IXOPAY-based payments platform (Transaction/Provisioning/Settlement/Gateway APIs) using Basic Auth and HMAC signing.

auth_methods.notes: MCP FX Audit tool: Bearer token via IPAYX_API_KEY env var, optional, required only for live provider quotes/full reports.
IXOPAY Transaction/Provisioning/Settlement APIs use HTTP Basic Auth (Base64 of username:password).
IXOPAY Gateway API requires HMAC-SHA hash signature in Authorization header.

self_serve_notes: Core FX audit MCP tools (mid-market rate lookups) work with no signup/API key. Free API key obtainable via iPayX website for live provider comparisons and forensic reports; some advanced reporting is billed per report. IXOPAY platform credential provisioning process not detailed in evidence (likely partner/admin setup given Basic Auth username+password model typical of PSP platforms).

api_surface.notes: MCP server exposes 4 tools (audit_fx_fee, compare_providers, score_invoice, get_mid_market_rate / check_fx_rate). Underlying IXOPAY platform has separate REST APIs: Transaction, Provisioning, Settlement, and Gateway APIs.

evidence.supports: MCP server exists, free/no-signup basic usage, tools list, live endpoint URL
MCP server package (@ipayx/mcp-fx-audit) exposing 4 FX audit tools
Third-party MCP registry listing confirming existence of iPayX MCP server
IXOPAY Transaction API uses Basic Auth
IXOPAY Provisioning API uses Basic Auth
IXOPAY Settlement API uses Basic Auth
API key/username setup for Basic Auth credentials
Gateway API requires HMAC signature in Authorization header

flags: Relationship between iPayX and IXOPAY platform is unclear from evidence - may be a different/underlying vendor rather than iPayX's own API
MCP server appears to be a lightweight community/vendor-built wrapper around FX rate comparisons, not a full payments API toolkit
Credential provisioning process for IXOPAY Basic Auth (self-serve vs partnership) not specified in evidence
Live endpoint URL differs between README (wild-bird-a412.ybolduc.workers.dev) and stated official endpoint (mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp), suggesting in-progress migration
